content.problemStatement:
Sidaga calls a sequence beautiful if the sum of elements of this sequence is strictly positive.

Kaspers gave Sidaga a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and asked him to partition it into exactly KKK non-empty **contiguous** subsequences such that each element of AAA belongs to exactly one subsequence and all KKK subsequences are beautiful.

Sidaga is getting ready for his wedding - he is busy learning how to dance, so he cannot solve this problem. Can you help him?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to partition AAA into KKK contiguous subsequences or `"NO"` otherwise.

If it is possible to partition AAA, print a second line containing KKK space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BKB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_KB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BK‚Äã denoting the sizes of the subsequences, where 1‚â§Bi1 \le B_i1‚â§Bi‚Äã for each valid iii and B1+B2+‚Ä¶+BK=NB_1 + B_2 + \ldots + B_K = NB1‚Äã+B2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+BK‚Äã=N. The first B1B_1B1‚Äã elements of AAA belong to the first subsequence, the next B2B_2B2‚Äã elements belong to the second subsequence and so on.

If there is more than one solution, you may print any one.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:
In first case, there are more than one options. Another such valid option is {2,2}.
In second case, it is not possible.
